mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuation through a lower discount rate and weaker cash yields, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next month when the policy rate is declining.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not above their unconditional median in periods where FEDFUNDS is falling over the prior 1-3 months, or if the sign flips negative in more than 40% of such observations.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is elevated and CPIAUCSL continues to rise, real policy rates tighten and multiple compression should dominate, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > CPIAUCSL inflation trend proxy is rising over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the average return is not below zero despite elevated rates and rising CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE reflects weakening labor income expectations and higher recession risk, so a sustained increase in unemployment should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE increases of more than 0.3 percentage points in 3 months are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns at least 60% of the time, or if the mean forward return is nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth in PAYEMS confirms broad income support and domestic demand resilience, which should lift equity expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when employment keeps expanding.
failure_condition: Reject if three-month streaks of PAYEMS growth are not associated with positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the conditional average return is below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and HOUST signals that consumer spending and housing demand are strengthening together, which should improve revenue expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive 3-month changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce higher 60-day SPX returns than either series alone, or if conditional returns are not positive on average.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and operating leverage in the real economy, which should support equities with a lag and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend INDPRO readings are not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the average conditional return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth weakens while UNRATE rises, the combined signal identifies a real activity slowdown that should depress earnings expectations and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns negative while UNRATE rises over the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if the joint deterioration in GDPC1 and UNRATE does not correspond to negative 60-day SPX returns in a majority of triggered cases, or if returns are positive on average.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in CPIAUCSL without a matching rise in FEDFUNDS eases real-rate pressure and supports valuation expansion, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next week in disinflationary pauses.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL monthly inflation trend falls for 2 consecutive months and FEDFUNDS is unchanged
failure_condition: Reject if two-month disinflation with unchanged FEDFUNDS is not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the conditional mean return is not above zero.
